docs.title: Polydesk
docs.text:
Polydesk is a professional terminal workspace for Polymarket.

It is starting as a read-only TUI focused on market discovery, watchlists, order books, and fast keyboard workflows. The long-term direction is broader: account views, orders, positions, rewards, trading workflows, and pluggable quantitative scripts with explicit safety boundaries.

docs.title: Current Shape
docs.text:
• Curses-based terminal UI with a plotext full-chart workspace.
  • Empty watchlist on startup.
  • Search and recommendation candidates loaded on demand.
  • Watchlist, order-book, CLOB detail, price history, discovery, and text queries run in background workers.
  • Modal command line inspired by Vim and Helix, with fuzzy command suggestions, aliases, command history, and Tab completion.
  • Layout navigation inspired by lazygit.
  • Read-only Polymarket CLI coverage for markets, CLOB data, events, series, tags, leaderboard, public profiles, comments, holders, open interest, and event volume.

docs.title: What You Can Do Today
docs.text:
• Search markets with :search <query> or load recommendations with :recommend.
  • Add interesting candidates to a local in-memory watchlist with :add <n|id|slug>.
  • Browse Market Detail and a side-by-side Ask/Bid depth ladder for the active YES or NO token.
  • Use :chart to plot a 15-minute default window of trade-price history with rolling Best Bid and Best Ask overlays in the full right-side workspace.
  • Press Enter or Tab to focus right-side detail/output panes, then scroll them with Up/Down or mouse wheel; press Esc to return to navigation.
  • Run read-only CLOB commands such as :mid, :spread, :history, :negrisk, and :detail.
  • Explore active events, event markets, series, and tags with :events, :event, :series, and :tags.
  • Query public/community data with :lb, :profile, :comments, :holders, :oi, and :evolume.

docs.title: Design Goals
docs.text: Polydesk should feel like a tool for repeated professional use, not a demo dashboard. The interface favors dense information, predictable keyboard flow, and clear separation between read-only analysis and any future wallet-mutating action.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Polydesk is intentionally split into small modules so future Polymarket features can be added without turning the TUI into one large file.

docs.title: Modules
docs.text: Path Responsibility polymarket_tui.py Thin executable entrypoint polytui/app.py Application state, input handling, commands, task coordination polytui/commands.py Command registry, aliases, fuzzy suggestions, completion, abbreviation resolution polytui/navigation.py Navigation pane registry and focus order polytui/ui/layout.py Pure layout geometry for the lazygit-style navigation shell polytui/ui/curses_view.py Curses rendering, layout, and plotext ANSI-to-curses chart rendering polytui/workers.py Background worker functions polytui/polymarket_cli.py Official CLI adapter and market parsing polytui/ui/palette.py Catppuccin Frappé colour palette (hex → curses RGB) polytui/models.py Shared domain models polytui/config.py Refresh and timeout constants

docs.title: Background Work
docs.text: Network and CLI calls must not block key handling. Search, recommendations, watchlist refreshes, live order-book polling, CLOB detail, price history, discovery, and generic text queries run through a ThreadPoolExecutor and return into the main UI loop through completed futures. The chart history worker requests dense trade-price data; plotext combines its selected time window with retained live best bid/ask snapshots.

docs.title: Future Extension Points
docs.text:
• Account layout.
  • Orders layout.
  • Positions layout.
  • Rewards layout.
  • Strategy/script workspace.
  • Confirmation gates for mutating actions.

docs.title: Python Environment
docs.text:
Use Python 3.11 or newer. You can manage the environment with venv, conda, uv, or another local Python environment tool.

Install the runtime and documentation dependencies:

python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Run the TUI
docs.text:
From the project root:

./polymarket_tui.py

The app expects the official polymarket CLI to be available on PATH.

docs.title: First Session
docs.text:
1. Start the TUI.
  2. Press : to enter command mode.
  3. Run :rec to load recommended open-market candidates into the Candidates list.
  4. Run :c or press 2 to focus Candidates, then use up/down in the left navigation column to browse.
  5. Run :a 1 to add the first candidate to the Watchlist.
  6. Use left/right to move between navigation panes: Watchlist, Candidates, Orders, Account, Strategies.
  7. Use 1–5 to jump directly to a navigation pane.
  8. When Watchlist or Candidates is focused, up/down selects a market and the right-side detail/order book update automatically.
  9. The Order Book panel polls the active token every 2 seconds and refreshes its best bid, best ask, and depth levels. If one side has no available level, it remains empty.
  10. Press Enter or Tab to focus Order Book, then use up/down to scroll the depth table and Esc to return to navigation-pane movement.
  11. Run :chart for a full-height chart. It defaults to a 15-minute window with historical trade price plus live Bid/Ask overlays. Use [ / ] to change its 30s, 1m, 5m, or 15m range, or specify it explicitly, for example :chart 5m. Run :book to return to the depth table.
  12. The focused navigation pane has the highlighted selection.
  13. Press ? to see all keybindings.

docs.title: Market Research
docs.text:
:s bitcoin
:a 1
:y
:mid
:spread
:history 1d 30
:detail

This searches for active Bitcoin markets, adds the first candidate to Watchlist, switches to the YES order book, and opens read-only CLOB data in the right-side output area.

docs.title: Discovery
docs.text:
:events
:event <event_id>
:series
:tags

:events shows active events in the left discovery area. :event <event_id> loads the event's markets into Candidates so they can be browsed and added to Watchlist.

docs.title: Community And Data Checks
docs.text:
:lb
:profile <address>
:comments market <market_id>
:holders
:oi
:evolume <event_id>

These commands are public/read-only. :holders and :oi default to the selected market's condition ID when one is available.

docs.title: Read-Only Scope
docs.text: The current TUI does not load .env, does not read private keys, and does not submit orders. It only calls read-only Polymarket CLI commands for markets, order books, CLOB data, discovery, community data, and public on-chain-style data exposed by the CLI.

docs.title: Interaction Model
docs.text:
Polydesk uses a modal, lazygit-style navigation layout: the left column is the primary interaction surface, and the right side renders context for the focused navigation pane.

┌────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┐
│  NAVIGATION        │  CONTEXT VIEW                      │
│  1 Watchlist       │  Market Detail                     │
│  market rows       │                                    │
│  2 Candidates      │  Order Book                        │
│  market rows       │                                    │
│  3 Orders          │  or pane-specific content          │
│  4 Account         │                                    │
│  5 Strategies      │                                    │
└────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: Theme
docs.text: All colours use the Catppuccin Frappé palette via curses.init_color(). The 8 standard curses colour indices are reassigned to precise Frappé RGB values at startup, giving a consistent look across terminals. If a terminal does not support can_change_color(), the TUI falls back to the standard curses palette.

docs.title: Normal Mode
docs.text:
Normal mode is for navigation-pane movement, row selection, and context scrolling.

Key Action Left / Right Move focus to previous / next navigation pane when navigation owns focus 1–5 Jump directly to Watchlist, Candidates, Orders, Account, or Strategies Enter / Tab Enter or cycle the right-side Market Detail / Order Book scroll focus Shift+Tab Cycle the right-side scroll focus backward Esc Return from right-side scroll focus to navigation focus Up / Down In Watchlist/Candidates: select a market. In right-side focus: scroll content [ / ] When the full Chart workspace has focus, decrease / increase its Bid/Ask sampling range g / G First / last market in the focused market pane / Enter command mode with search prefilled : Enter command mode ? Toggle help

Focus indicator: the left navigation border is bright while navigation owns focus. Selected market rows are highlighted only when their navigation pane owns focus. Enter or Tab can temporarily move focus to Market Detail or Order Book for scrolling; Esc returns to navigation focus.

docs.title: Command Mode
docs.text:
Press : to enter command mode. The command picker uses fuzzy matching and shows a vertical suggestion popup. Tab cycles suggestions, Up/Down moves through suggestions and scrolls the list when needed, Enter runs the selected command, and Esc cancels command mode. A command with no match remains visible with an explicit error prompt until it is submitted or cancelled.

Command names support aliases and unique-prefix abbreviations. For example, :s, :search, and a unique fuzzy match for search resolve to the same command.

docs.title: Core Commands
docs.text: Command Aliases Action :search <query> :s, :find Search active market candidates :recommend :rec, :recommended Load recommended open-market candidates :watchlist :w, :watch Focus Watchlist :candidates :c, :results Focus Candidates :pane <name> :nav, :p Focus watchlist, candidates, orders, account, or strategies :workspace <name> :ws Compatibility alias for pane focus :add <n|id|slug> :a Add a candidate to Watchlist :remove <n|id|slug> :rm, :delete Remove a watched market :refresh :ref, :reload Refresh the current market view :yes :y Show the YES order book :no :n Show the NO order book :chart [30s\|1m\|5m\|15m] :ch Replace the market context with a full rolling Best Bid / Best Ask chart :book :ob, :orderbook Return to the Market Detail and Order Book depth layout :market :m, :open Show selected market identifiers :help :h, :? Toggle help :quit :q, :exit, :wq Exit Polydesk

docs.title: CLOB Commands
docs.text:
These commands operate on the currently selected market and active YES/NO token.

Command Aliases Action :mid :midpoint Show midpoint price :spread :spr Show bid-ask spread :history [interval] [fidelity] :hist, :ph Show price history; default is 1d 30 :negrisk :nr Show negative-risk status :detail :dt Show full CLOB detail for the active token

docs.title: Discovery Commands
docs.text: Command Aliases Action :events [tag] [limit] :ev List active events, optionally filtered by tag :event <id> :e Load an event and show its markets in Candidates :series [limit] :ser List series :tags :tag List tags :disc :discovery Toggle the discovery view

docs.title: Community And Public Data Commands
docs.text: Command Aliases Action :leaderboard [period] [order_by] [limit] :lb, :top Show trader leaderboard output :profile <address> :pf, :user Look up a public profile :comments <event|market> <id> :cmts List comments for an event or market :holders [condition_id] :hld Show top token holders; defaults to selected market condition ID :oi [condition_id] :openinterest Show open interest; defaults to selected market condition ID :evolume <event_id> :evol Show event volume

docs.title: Active List
docs.text: When Watchlist or Candidates is focused, the right-side context view shows Market Detail and Order Book for the selected market. Search/recommend focuses Candidates; adding/removing markets returns focus to Watchlist when appropriate. Text output and price history temporarily occupy the lower right-side slot normally used by Order Book. Orders, Account, and Strategies are routed read-only panes for now.

docs.title: Live Bid/Ask Chart
docs.text:
The full Chart workspace combines available trade-price history with locally retained Best Bid / Best Ask samples for the active YES or NO token:

  • Green B line: highest available bid.
  • Red A line: lowest available ask.
  • Cyan History line: historical trade prices from CLOB. It is not a reconstructed historical bid or ask.
  • Live refresh interval: 2 seconds, using read-only polymarket clob book <token> -o json polling.

Run :chart to open the full workspace. It uses plotext Braille markers to render a thin terminal-native graph with a cyan trade-price baseline and green/red Best Bid/Ask lines. :chart 5m selects an explicit rolling range; without an argument it uses 15m. With Chart focused, [ and ] switch among 30s, 1m, 5m, and 15m. Samples arrive every 2 seconds and remain available for the selected token until the market or YES/NO side changes. The history worker requests a dense one-hour CLOB price series, then the chart keeps only the current range. Use :book to restore the depth table. The graph is terminal-rendered rather than a Matplotlib bitmap, but its time and price coordinates use the source values directly. When CLOB reports that a token has no order book, the panel shows an unavailable state and waits 60 seconds before retrying automatically; use :ref to retry immediately.

docs.title: Depth Ladder
docs.text:
The standard Order Book view presents the best asks and bids side by side on each depth row. Ask size/price appear on the left in red; bid price/size appear on the right in green. This keeps the two sides comparable without scrolling between separate sections.

Use :chart for a full-height chart workspace. It replaces both Market Detail and Order Book depth with the live bid/ask chart so that the time and price axes have the full right-side workspace. Use :book to restore the standard market detail and depth layout. The chart remains terminal-rendered, so it is numerically time/price accurate but does not emulate Matplotlib's pixel-level anti-aliasing.

docs.title: Bottom UI
docs.text:
• Statusline: current mode, active list + position, pending work, refresh cadence.
  • Promptline: command input or short status/error messages.
  • Completion strip: command suggestions shown above the statusline while typing.

docs.title: Near Term
docs.text:
• Persist watchlists between sessions.
  • Add stronger discovery selection flows for events, series, and tags.
  • Improve text-output paging and mouse-region handling.
  • Add configuration for refresh cadence and UI theme.

docs.title: Professional Workflows
docs.text:
• Account overview.
  • Balances and allowances.
  • Open orders.
  • Positions and PnL.
  • Rewards tracking.
  • Deeper event-level market grouping.

docs.title: Quantitative Scripts
docs.text:
Polydesk should eventually allow strategy scripts to plug into the workspace. This should happen behind clear boundaries:

  • Read-only script mode first.
  • Explicit capabilities declaration.
  • No hidden wallet access.
  • Confirmation gates before any trading or wallet mutation.
  • Audit-friendly logs for generated intents and submitted actions.

docs.title: Safety Model
docs.text: Polydesk is being built around a strict separation between read-only analysis and wallet-mutating actions.

docs.title: Current Guarantees
docs.text:
The current TUI:

  • Does not load .env.
  • Does not read POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY.
  • Does not create or cancel orders.
  • Does not approve tokens.
  • Does not bridge, split, merge, or redeem CTF positions.
  • Uses only read-only Polymarket CLI calls for market browsing, CLOB inspection, discovery, community data, and public data views.

docs.title: Future Mutating Features
docs.text:
Trading, approvals, wallet mutation, and quantitative script execution must be gated behind explicit user confirmation. Future UI should clearly show:

  • What action will be submitted.
  • Which wallet/account is involved.
  • Maximum token or USDC exposure.
  • Whether the action is simulated, signed, or broadcast.
  • The exact command or API call behind the operation.

docs.title: Secret Handling
docs.text: Secrets belong only in local secret storage such as .env or the official Polymarket CLI config. They must never be committed, copied into docs, or stored under .agents/.
